extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t22 (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage>=6). To trigger it I must reach opted_out=true and stage==6. Key observations: (1) the stage-advancing transitions t3,t6,t9,t12,t15,t18 (each on key_2) are guarded only by the stage value, not by opted_out, so stage can be advanced regardless of opt-out status; (2) optout (t1) sets opted_out=true and sent=false but leaves stage unchanged. Counterexample (8 events, within the 9-event bound): six key_2 events advance stage 0->1->2->3->4->5->6 (sent stays false), then optout sets opted_out=true (sent=false, stage stays 6), then send_attempt: t2 is disabled (not opted_out is false), t21 is disabled (stage<6 is false since stage==6), and t22 is enabled (opted_out and stage>=6 both true), so t22 fires setting sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=6 — a reachable state where opted_out is true and sent is true, contradicting the property.
